updates.note: On May 18, 2026, the Backward Classes Welfare Department issued Notification No. 945-BCW/MR-23/2026 implementing the Calcutta High Court's May 2024 order by restricting eligibility to 66 pre-2010 OBC classes under a unified 7% reservation for state services and posts. This officially withdraws the earlier 17% quota structure comprising Category A (10%) and Category B (7%) sub-categories, reverting the state's reservation regime to the pre-2010 framework.

sources.name: Backward Classes Welfare Department · Notification No. 945-BCW/MR-23/2026 · May 18, 2026

additionalNotes.label: Criticism
additionalNotes.text: Opposition parties and Muslim groups strongly criticized the decision to scrap the post-2010 OBC sub-categories. They argued that reducing the total OBC quota from 17% to 7% structurally deprives numerous communities of their reservation benefits.
